THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AS IMPROPER CAUSES OF ACTION UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES HERE. calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Dec-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS P. TUNNY OF REUBEN, JUNIUS & ROSE LLP, TEL. NO. 567-9000 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. SUSAN CLEVELAND-KNOWLES & ATTY. KRISTEN A. JENSEN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-6769 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action. First, Petitioner's satisfaction of the Mitigation Fee Act requirements is not dispositive of whether Petitioner exhausted its administrative remedies. Rather, if administrative remedies are provided for Petitioner's protest, Petitioner must allege exhaustion thereof. However, it is not clear from either the face of the Complaint or the code provisions in Respondents' Request for Judicial Notice that the Building Code or Planning Code provide any administrative remedies for Petitioner's protest of the fee based on the grounds asserted in the Petition. Second, nothing in Respondents' Request for Judicial Notice conflicts with Petitioner's claim that the Fee Deferral Program was intended to establish a separate fee structure for those projects. Further, the language contained on the Fee Reports is not dispositive of whether the fee charged here was permissible under the Building and Planning Codes. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action for declaratory and injunctive relief as improper causes of action under the circumstances here. (See Zetterberg v. State Dept. of Public Health (1974) 43 Cal.App.3d 657, 663 ["A difference of opinion as to the interpretation of a statute as between a citizen and a governmental agency does not give rise to a justiciable controversy [for declaratory relief]."; Tejon Real Estate, LLC v. City of Los Angeles (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 149,154-55.) Additionally, Petitioner fails to allege or argue in opposition that its legal remedies are inadequate. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Oct-07-2014 Continued To Dec-04-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
